sections.paragraph: μετρητής, οῦ, ὁ [μετρέω] ‘a utensil capable of measuring about 40 liters of liquid’, a metretes, known as the Heb. bath holding about 39 liters, hence in J 2:6, by metonymy of content for measurer of content, each water pot has capacity of up to two or three metretes, i.e. 18 to 27 gallons.
